title: try_load_wallet_fvks: comment wrong about empty passphrase semantics
description: The code comment says 'Empty passphrase means the user left the field blank — wallet will be unavailable for this session.' This is wrong: if the wallet was created with --no-passphrase, decrypt_wallet_key('') succeeds and the wallet IS available. The comment describes the no-passphrase-protected case only. What it should say: 'If the wallet was created with --no-passphrase, an empty passphrase here will successfully decrypt it. If a passphrase was set, an empty string here will fail decryption and the wallet will be unavailable.' A future maintainer reading the current comment will think pressing Enter always disables the wallet, and might introduce a short-circuit on empty passphrase that breaks no-passphrase wallets.
